_comment: Claude Code's native HTTP+OAuth MCP transport. Claude Code looks for `.mcp.json` at the plugin root by convention and reads this file directly — no bridge subprocess needed. The OAuth flow on first invocation opens a localhost:3118 callback, the user pastes their cp_… token in the consent page, and Claude Code caches the resulting access token in its own auth store. Codex (which only speaks stdio MCP) reads `./.codex-plugin/mcp-servers.json` instead — that file uses mcp-remote to bridge stdio↔HTTP to the same hosted server.
